title: Maps, Strings, Bytes, Runes, Unicode, and Deterministic Results · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A support operations lead. The lead needs deterministic counts and summaries from multilingual incident records without aliasing input or confusing bytes, runes, and user-visible text. Planned artifact: Deliver Unicode-Safe Incident Summarizer with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict whether append reuses or replaces storage and use copy or clone patterns to establish ownership.
Create, update, delete, clear, and query maps while distinguishing missing keys from stored zero values.
Decode UTF-8 with range and unicode/utf8 while handling byte indexes, replacement runes, invalid input, and grapheme limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
project case — Incident labels include "café", "café", "東京", invalid UTF-8, and 4 repeated ASCII keys. Count without confusing missing and zero, report byte and rune evidence, then sort keys for deterministic output.
